functional.objective: Diagnose Neon/Prisma drift without exposing secrets or mutating production
partial: Vercel session and encrypted Neon/DB variables confirmed by name only; local environment has no DATABASE_URL; Prisma schema validates locally.
testsRun: vercel env ls: DATABASE_URL and Neon-related variables exist as encrypted values
testsPending: Read-only prisma migrate status with ephemeral DATABASE_URL/DIRECT_URL

blockers.description: Neon/Prisma drift read-only check requires a secure non-persisted DATABASE_URL or DIRECT_URL. Vercel has encrypted DB/Neon variables by name, but local env has no DB URL and vercel env pull would write secrets to a file.
blockers.impact: Cannot prove Neon migration drift status from this session without violating the no-secret-files rule or receiving an ephemeral DB URL. No production mutation is authorized.
blockers.recommendedAction: Provide DATABASE_URL/DIRECT_URL via ephemeral environment input or authorize a secure retrieval path that does not write credentials to repo, logs, artifacts, prompts, or reports.
blockers.unblockCondition: Read-only prisma migrate status completes with DATABASE_URL supplied ephemerally; any production mutation has explicit human approval.

reasons: Neon/Prisma drift read-only check requires a secure non-persisted DATABASE_URL or DIRECT_URL. Vercel has encrypted DB/Neon variables by name, but local env has no DB URL and vercel env pull would write secrets to a file.

resume.exactPoint: Await secure non-persisted DB URL input or approved secret retrieval path

preconditions: Confirm no handoff supersedes this one
